description: A specialized museum showcasing the history and culture of the Six Dynasties, featuring modern architectural design.
A commercial street district with a Republic of China-era ambiance, offering dining, entertainment, and shopping; its vibrant night scenery makes it a popular gathering spot for young people.
A double-deck bridge designed and constructed independently by China, serving both railway and roadway purposes, and a city landmark.
The mother river of Nanjing, where night cruises on painted boats offer views of ancient architectural clusters along both banks.
A famous snack street in Nanjing, gathering authentic local delicacies and recommended by locals as a food destination.
Showcasing the intangible cultural heritage of Nanjing Yunjin brocade, with demonstrations of the weaving process.

attraction_name: Music Stage of Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum Scenic Area in Zhongshan Scenic Area
description: An open-air music stage built during the Republic of China era, featuring exquisite architecture and a serene, elegant surrounding environment.
A double-spiral pedestrian bridge over the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark offering views of the river and the city's skyline.
A sacred site commemorating China's New Democratic Revolution, a solemn place to honor revolutionary martyrs.
A famous ancient temple in Nanjing, now a national revolutionary martyr cemetery, known for its tranquil environment and the iconic Linggu Pagoda.
A sightseeing experience on the Qinhuai River, offering views of ancient buildings and night scenery along the banks, capturing the charm of the ancient Six Dynasties capital.
The ruins of a Southern Dynasties temple, now a cemetery for fallen soldiers of the National Revolutionary Army, with the Linggu Pagoda as a symbolic structure.
A cluster of Republic of China-era mansions, a well-preserved historic district with elegant architectural styles.
Nanjing's top-tier shopping center, featuring internationally renowned brands, located at the heart of the Xinjiekou commercial district.
China's first national highest academic institution, a core scenic area of the Qinhuai River scenic belt, with well-preserved ancient architectural clusters.
A preserved district of Ming and Qing dynasty architecture, blending traditional residences with modern commerce, offering an experience of old Nanjing's charm.
The site where the Treaty of Nanking was signed, an important witness to modern history.

attraction_name: Purple Mountain Observatory of the Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: The birthplace of modern Chinese astronomy, offering panoramic views of Nanjing and serving as a science education base.
